spr_candidate_list_summary.source: SENARAI CALON LAYAK _PRU DN N.SEMBILAN KE-15_OD_v2.xlsx (SPR Official)
spr_candidate_list_summary.key_finding: BN-PN ELECTORAL PACT VERIFIED — NO seat has both a BN AND a PN candidate (zero BN-vs-PN overlap). HOWEVER, BERSATU contests SEPARATELY from PN under its own banner, going head-to-head against PN in 8 seats AND against BN in 16 seats. Bersatu faces an opposition rival (BN or PN) in ALL 24 of its seats. Bersatu is a fully independent THIRD FORCE fighting everyone. 25 of 36 seats (69%) are multi-cornered (3+ candidates) — driven by Bersatu's breakaway.

positive.entity: Pakatan Harapan (PH) — INDIRECT BENEFICIARY of opposition split
positive.rationale: MAJOR STRATEGIC REVERSAL in PH's favor. Yesterday (20260717) PH was reduced to the opposition's TARGET (5 occurrences, all defeat-framing, negative) as the opposition 'unified' via the BN-PN pact. Today (NOMINATION DAY), the SPR candidate list reveals the opposition has FRACTURED into THREE forces (BN 25, PN 11, BERSATU 24) — and Bersatu is contesting AGAINST both BN (16 seats) and PN (8 seats). 25 of 36 seats are now MULTI-CORNERED (3+ candidates). The very vote-splitting the BN-PN pact was designed to ELIMINATE (yesterday: 'Jika berpecah akhirnya kita akan kalah' — if votes split, we lose) has been RE-CREATED by Bersatu's breakaway. The INCUMBENT (PH) is the chief structural beneficiary of 3-cornered fights that split the opposition vote. PH has also CONFIRMED its full 36-candidate slate (one per DUN). This is a return toward PH's 20260715 position (first-mover advantage) — the opposition's self-inflicted fragmentation restores PH's structural edge. NOTE: PH still has minimal narrative voice in the corpus (6 occurrences, nst+utusan), but its position has improved structurally. MB Aminuddin's Linggi candidacy (see below) adds a governance narrative.
positive.confidence: HIGH — SPR verified candidate list (ground truth) + multi-source editorial confirmation of the split

narrative_indicators.indicator: Bersatu-PN FRACTURE on Nomination Day — Opposition Splits into Three Forces
narrative_indicators.type: Electoral — coalition fracture (the dominant story today)
narrative_indicators.trend: MAJOR ESCALATION — 20260717: PN internal friction (Muhyiddin vs Ahmad Samsuri, MEDIUM, 2 occ) → 20260718: FULL BERSATU-PN SPLIT (Bersatu contests 24 seats separately, 16 occ, Muhyiddin calls PN 'toxic')
narrative_indicators.evidence: SPR list: Bersatu 24 seats (own banner) vs PN 11 seats — 8 head-to-head Bersatu-vs-PN seats. NST: 'Muhyiddin: PN toxic under PAS leadership, Bersatu eyes new coalition'; The Star: 'Negri polls: Rift widens between PAS, Bersatu' (coalition partners poised to clash in 8 seats); NST: 'Bersatu to face PAS, Wawasan in eight seats'; utusan: 'Bersatu bertanding 24 kerusi untuk nilai penerimaan pengundi'
narrative_indicators.significance: The SINGLE most significant development of the cycle. Yesterday's 'opposition unity achieved' (ESC-006 RESOLVED) is REVERSED — the opposition has FRACTURED. Bersatu, formerly PN's largest Malay component, is now a fully independent third force contesting 24 seats against BN (16) and PN (8). This recreates the vote-splitting the BN-PN pact was designed to eliminate. 25 of 36 seats are multi-cornered. The INCUMBENT (PH) is the chief structural beneficiary. This is the second 180-degree reversal in 72h. Muhyiddin escalates from 'claim rebutted' (20260717) to calling PN 'toxic' and announcing a new bloc.

narrative_indicators.indicator: BN-PN Bilateral Pact VERIFIED Intact — But Broader Opposition Unity Broken
narrative_indicators.trend: VERIFIED — 20260717: BN-PN pact claimed (unverified) → 20260718: SPR list CONFIRMS zero BN-vs-PN overlap (ground truth). Pact holds bilaterally but the broader 3-way opposition unity does NOT.
narrative_indicators.evidence: SPR candidate list: 25 BN + 11 PN candidates, ZERO seats with both BN and PN. NST: 'Negri polls to test BN-PN electoral pact against PH'; utusan: 'Kejayaan rundingan pembahagian kerusi antara BN dan PN'.
narrative_indicators.significance: The bilateral BN-PN electoral pact is VERIFIED at ground-truth level (SPR official list). BN and PN honor their no-overlap agreement. However, this bilateral pact is now only PART of the opposition picture — Bersatu (24 seats) sits OUTSIDE it and contests both. So 'opposition unity' = BN+PN (36 seats, no overlap) BUT minus Bersatu (24 seats fighting both). The 20260717 '36 opposition candidates matching PH's slate' claim is now WRONG — total opposition candidates are 25+11+24 = 60, far exceeding PH's 36, and they fight EACH OTHER in 24 seats.

narrative_indicators.evidence: NST: 'Muhyiddin says Bersatu remains sole opposition voice'; NST (JEMPOL): 'Hadi dismisses Muhyiddin's toxic PN claim, points finger at Bersatu'; Utusan (verbatim): Samsuri — 'dakwaan ... adalah tidak berasas' + 'Proses ... diluluskan oleh Majlis Tertinggi PN'; nomination-day-summary-1633: PN info chief floats termination of Bersatu's membership.
narrative_indicators.significance: The 08:31 'Muhyiddin isolated' framing is SUPERSEDED. The Bersatu-PN split is now a public, mutual, leadership-level war (chairman vs president, PAS president vs Bersatu president), with a membership-termination threat floated. This is the most opposition-cohesion-negative development of the cycle and maximally favourable to the incumbent (PH).
narrative_indicators.revision3_update: REFINED — the split is now THREE-WAY: Bersatu vs (PN+PAS). Hadi Awang (JEMPOL, NST) confirms PAS is now the third party in the confrontation (defends PN/PAS, attacks Bersatu). The 16:33 'mutual Muhyiddin-vs-Samsuri' framing is extended to a Bersatu-vs-(PN+PAS) war. Cycle 3 escalation: PN 'has grounds to remove Bersatu' (Kiandee, NST) — see NAR-18.

narrative_indicators.indicator: BN-PN 'understanding, not merger' — synchronised containment re-framing by 4 BN leaders
narrative_indicators.trend: NEW — ESC-009 downgraded from 'verified pact' (08:31) to informal BN-PAS 'understanding'. BN leadership abandons the 'unified opposition / 36 matching candidates' framing.
narrative_indicators.evidence: Zahid (REMBAU, NST): 'BN-PN cooperation based on understanding, not formal pact'; Wee/MCA (NST+Star): 'understanding ... not a merger'; Johari (TAMPIN, NST): 'BN-PN cooperation depends on common ground, not ideology'; Asyraf Wajdi (bharian): 'Kesefahaman BN-PN demi kestabilan politik'.
narrative_indicators.significance: A coordinated containment strategy — likely responding to (a) Bersatu's split fracturing the 'unified opposition' claim and (b) UMNO grassroots dissatisfaction (ESC-012). The bilateral arrangement holds but is re-framed as informal; the pact's strategic PURPOSE (unify to defeat PH) is publicly softened by BN itself.

narrative_indicators.indicator: PH proactive narrative EMERGES — governance (Fahmi) + strategy (Loke) + integrity (Fuziah/PKR)
narrative_indicators.type: Political — incumbent narrative (fills prior data gap)
narrative_indicators.trend: NEW — 08:31 ('No PH proactive campaign narrative', data gap) → 16:33 (structured governance+integrity+strategy narrative across NST/bharian/Astro Awani).
narrative_indicators.evidence: NST (JEMPOL): 'PH banking on Aminuddin's MB track record, says Fahmi'; bharian: 'Kejayaan pentadbiran PH, keupayaan Aminuddin fokus kempen'; NST (JELEBU): 'Negri polls will test claims DAP is losing support, says Loke'; Astro Awani: 'Kerusi DUN di Jelebu kritikal tentukan pembentukan kerajaan negeri' (Loke); Astro Awani: Fuziah Salleh 'Gadai kepercayaan demi kemenangan tiada dalam kamus strategi kami'.
narrative_indicators.significance: PH's structural advantage (opposition split) is now NARRATIVE-backed. The 08:31 caveat that PH's benefit was 'structural, not narrative' is SUPERSEDED. Three PH voices (Fahmi/Loke/Fuziah) provide governance, strategic, and integrity frames. PH's edge is reinforced.

narrative_indicators.indicator: 'Malay tsunami forming in Negri Sembilan?' — analyst narrative (new PH-risk vector)
narrative_indicators.trend: NEW — introduces upward pressure on ESC-007 (PH Malay seats) but structurally counteracted by the Bersatu-PN split.
narrative_indicators.evidence: The Star analysis: 'Malay tsunami forming in Negri Sembilan?' — posits Malay-vote consolidation as the decisive dynamic.
narrative_indicators.significance: If Malay consolidation favours a unified BN-PN, it elevates the Tier-3/Tier-4 seats and threatens PH's 4 Malay seats (Klawang, Ampangan, Sikamat, Pilah). However, Bersatu splits the Malay vote in 20 of its 24 seats, counteracting consolidation. A watch item, not a confirmed shift.

narrative_indicators.indicator: EC cross-verified contest granularity — 12 PH-BN-Bersatu 3-cornered; Bersatu fights allies in 20/24 seats
narrative_indicators.trend: REFINED — 08:31 (25 multi-cornered, SPR-derived) → 16:33 (EC official: 21 three-cornered; 12 are PH-BN-Bersatu, cross-verified to SPR roll).
narrative_indicators.evidence: The Star (SEREMBAN, EC official): 'Total of 21 seats to see three-cornered contests' + 'PH, BN, Bersatu in three-cornered fights in 12 of the 36'. Cross-verified to SPR roll: 12 PH-BN-Bersatu (N02,N03,N06,N09,N12,N15,N16,N17,N20,N24,N28,N32) + 8 PH-BN-Bersatu-PN direct-clash (N04,N05,N13,N14,N23,N25,N31,N34) + 1 PH-PN-Ind (N30) = 21 three-cornered. Bersatu fights one/both allies in 20 of 24 seats.
narrative_indicators.significance: EC-official granularity confirms the opposition fragmentation is COMPREHENSIVE, not partial. Bersatu's independent-from-PN posture is verified across nearly all its seats.
narrative_indicators.revision3_update: CORROBORATED — NST independently confirms the EC's 21 three-cornered + 2 five-way figure: 'N9 polls: Three-cornered contests on the rise, two seats witness five-way fights'. The two five-way fights are N.10 Nilai and N.33 Sri Tanjung. PIR-04 lifted from 'EC single-source' to CONFIRMED (EC + NST mainstream). Bersatu's independent posture verified across nearly all seats.

Positive candidate-quality signal but in 3-cornered/5-way seats the opposition vote splits and advantages PH.

narrative_indicators.significance: Elevates the strategic stakes of the NS contest beyond a single state election — the BN/UMNO president publicly frames NS as a bellwether for national coalition restructuring (Melaka PRN + PRU16 lineups). Rhetorically adjacent to trigger #2 (alliance/realignment). Reinforces BN's 'understanding, not merger' containment (rev 2) while hedging toward post-election realignment. Watch for any concrete BN-PAS or BN-PH accommodation signal.

escalation_flags.category: Bersatu-PN FRACTURE — Opposition Splits into Three Forces on Nomination Day
escalation_flags.description: The standing opposition-unity flag (ESC-006, 'RESOLVED' on 20260717) is REVERSED. The SPR official candidate list reveals Bersatu is contesting 24 seats SEPARATELY from PN under its own BERSATU banner, going head-to-head against PN in 8 seats (Klawang, Serting, Sikamat, Ampangan, Mambau, Paroi, Bagan Pinang, Gemas) and against BN in 16 seats. Muhyiddin calls PN 'toxic' under PAS leadership and announces Bersatu will form a new coalition/bloc after the NS election. PAS is 'ready to face Bersatu.' The opposition has fractured from a unified BN+PN bloc (20260717) into THREE forces (BN 25, PN 11, Bersatu 24). 25 of 36 seats are multi-cornered. The Malay-vote-unification strategy is undermined by Bersatu's vote-splitting. The incumbent (PH) is the chief structural beneficiary.
escalation_flags.action: Track the 8 Bersatu-vs-PN seats for vote-splitting margins. Monitor whether Bersatu's new-bloc announcement triggers further coalition realignment. Watch for Bersatu-PN campaign clashes (social-media monitoring active). Assess whether Muhyiddin's contradictory messaging (toxic + new bloc + not quitting) stabilizes or escalates. Cross-reference 2023 SPR results for the 8 head-to-head seats.
escalation_flags.carries_forward: REVERSES ESC-006-20260717 (opposition unity, RESOLVED) — REOPENED. ESCALATES ESC-010-20260717 (PN internal friction, MEDIUM) to CRITICAL. SUPERSEDES ESC-009-20260717 (BN-PN pact) — bilateral pact holds but broader unity broken.
escalation_flags.revision2_update: DEEPENED to MUTUAL, ON-THE-RECORD confrontation. Hadi Awang (NST, JEMPOL) dismisses 'toxic PN' and points finger at Bersatu; Samsuri (Utusan verbatim) refutes Muhyiddin ('tidak berasas'); Muhyiddin (NST 083302) declares Bersatu 'sole opposition' and claims PN supports the government; PN info chief floats termination of Bersatu's membership (summary-1633). Severity remains CRITICAL; the fracture is now a public mutual leadership war, not a one-sided spat.
escalation_flags.revision3_update: TOWARD FORMALIZATION — 'PN has grounds to remove Bersatu, says Kiandee' (NST, Cycle 3/19:19). The mutual confrontation (rev 2) now threatens to formalize into a procedural expulsion during the campaign window. The termination threat has acquired concrete 'grounds' (progression: 'could face' -> 'info chief floats' -> 'has grounds'). The split is now THREE-WAY: Bersatu vs (PN+PAS) (Hadi, JEMPOL). Severity remains CRITICAL; the fracture is escalating toward a formal coalition-restructuring event (trigger #2 adjacent). See new ESC-014.

escalation_flags.status: REOPENED — the 'jual mahal' disruption (RESOLVED via BN-PN pact 20260717) is REPLACED by a larger disruption: Bersatu's full breakaway
escalation_flags.description: Yesterday ESC-006 was marked RESOLVED — BN-PN pact overcame the 'jual mahal' negotiation friction and 'opposition unity achieved.' Today the SPR list shows that while the BILATERAL BN-PN pact holds (zero overlap), the BROADER opposition unity is BROKEN — Bersatu (a PN component) has broken away and contests against both BN and PN. The vote-splitting the pact was designed to eliminate has been RECREATED at a larger scale (24 Bersatu seats vs the original 'jual mahal' negotiation friction). The 'Jika berpecah akhirnya kita akan kalah' (if votes split, we lose) warning from UMNO NS Sec (utusan) now applies to the opposition's OWN self-inflicted split.
escalation_flags.action: Flag REOPENED. Monitor for whether Bersatu withdraws from any of the 8 head-to-head seats (late withdrawal would re-unify). Track whether PN/Bersatu reach a last-minute accommodation. If the fracture holds through polling day, the opposition's Malay-vote-unification strategy has failed.
escalation_flags.carries_forward: ESC-006-20260717 (RESOLVED) → REOPENED. The original 'jual mahal' (ESC-006-20260714/15) is superseded by the Bersatu breakaway — a structurally larger disruption.

Only Pilah remains a clean 2-cornered BN-PH fight.
escalation_flags.action: Cross-reference 2023 SPR results for the 4 PH Malay seats' margins. Assess whether 3-cornered vote-splitting is enough for PH to retain Klawang/Ampangan/Sikamat. Track Pilah (the only clean 2-cornered PH Malay seat) as the highest-risk seat.

verdict:
NOMINATION DAY (18 Jul 2026) has produced the most consequential development of the cycle and the SECOND 180-degree reversal in 72h. The SPR OFFICIAL CANDIDATE LIST (103 candidates, 36 DUNs) — a verified ground-truth intelligence asset unavailable on prior days — reveals that the opposition has FRACTURED into THREE forces: BN (25 seats), PN (11 seats), and BERSATU (24 seats, contesting SEPARATELY under its own banner). The bilateral BN-PN electoral pact is VERIFIED intact (zero BN-vs-PN overlap), but the broader opposition unity — the dominant positive narrative of 20260717 ('opposition unity achieved', ESC-006 RESOLVED) — is BROKEN. Bersatu, formerly PN's largest Malay component, is now a fully independent third force contesting AGAINST PN in 8 seats (Klawang, Serting, Sikamat, Ampangan, Mambau, Paroi, Bagan Pinang, Gemas) and AGAINST BN in 16 seats. Muhyiddin Yassin calls PN 'toxic' under PAS leadership (NST), announces a new post-election coalition/bloc (The Star), then contradicts himself by insisting Bersatu is 'not quitting PN' (The Star). The result: 25 of 36 seats (69%) are multi-cornered (3+ candidates), recreating the very vote-splitting the BN-PN pact was designed to eliminate ('Jika berpecah akhirnya kita akan kalah'). The INCUMBENT (PH) is the chief structural beneficiary — PH's 20260717 position (reduced to opposition target) has improved back toward its 20260715 first-mover advantage, but via opposition self-fragmentation rather than PH's own action (PH has minimal narrative voice, 6 occ). MB Aminuddin's Linggi move is CONFIRMED (SPR: PH candidate N.32 Linggi) with positive governance framing. BN adds a heavyweight (Tok Mat / Mohamad Hasan for Rantau) but faces internal UMNO dissent (division leaders unhappy 11 seats given to PN). Net NS-relevant sentiment is BALANCED (4 positive / 4 neutral / 4 negative) — a shift from 20260717's opposition-positive (6/4/4) BACK toward a PH-favorable balance. The positive entities are the incumbent side (PH structural beneficiary, MB Aminuddin governance, nomination logistics, Tok Mat); the negative entities are ALL opposition-cohesion failures (Bersatu fracture, Muhyiddin escalation, opposition-unity broken, UMNO dissent). The dominant escalation is ESC-011 (CRITICAL, Bersatu-PN FRACTURE), which REVERSES ESC-006 (REOPENED) and ESCALATES ESC-010 (MEDIUM→CRITICAL). ESC-007 (PH Malay seats at risk) is PARTIALLY COUNTERED — 3 of PH's 4 Malay seats now have split opposition votes. Key intelligence uplift: SPR-verified candidate-party-seat mapping for ALL 36 DUNs (supersedes all prior media-reported candidate claims), confirmed polling day (1 Aug), confirmed MB Aminuddin Linggi move, Tok Mat Rantau candidacy, full contest-structure mapping (11 two-cornered / 21 three-cornered / 2 four-cornered / 2 five-cornered). The NS PRN has moved from a 'genuine two-sided contest' (20260717) to a FRAGMENTED multi-cornered contest favoring the incumbent.

[16:33Δ REVISION 2 — FULL-DAY DELTA MERGED] The later collection batches (raw-scrape _070937 + _083302 + the nomination-day PIR summaries) materially extend and reinforce the 08:31 Nomination-Day verdict, with three movements: (1) the Bersatu-PN fracture DEEPENS from a one-sided Muhyiddin spat (08:31) into a MUTUAL, ON-THE-RECORD leadership confrontation — Hadi Awang dismisses 'toxic PN' and points the finger at Bersatu (NST, JEMPOL); Samsuri verbatim-refutes Muhyiddin in Utusan ('tidak berasas' / 'diluluskan oleh Majlis Tertinggi PN'); Muhyiddin declares Bersatu the 'sole opposition' and claims PN supports the government (NST); a PN info chief floats terminating Bersatu's membership. ESC-011/ESC-010 remain CRITICAL and are now mutual. (2) PH's edge shifts from STRUCTURAL-only to STRUCTURAL + NARRATIVE: Fahmi ('banking on Aminuddin's MB track record'), Loke (NS-as-DAP-referendum + Jelebu-4-seats-critical), and Fuziah/PKR (integrity framing) give PH a proactive voice — FILLING the 08:31 'no PH narrative' gap. (3) BN leadership synchronises an 'understanding, not merger' containment re-framing (Zahid/Wee/Johari/Asyraf), DOWNGRADING ESC-009 from 'verified pact' to an informal BN-PAS understanding — BN itself abandons the 'unified opposition' framing, likely to contain fallout from the Bersatu split + UMNO divisional dissent. The clean nomination is upgraded to multi-source CONFIRMED (EC + PDRM; zero rejections, zero incidents). EC granularity (cross-verified): 21 three-cornered fights, 12 of them PH-BN-Bersatu; Bersatu fights one/both allies in 20 of 24 seats. New candidate-quality sweep (Faizal mature, Razali ex-police, LeeVinesh youngest, Ismail unfazed, Tok Mat heavyweight). The ONE new PH-risk vector is the analyst 'Malay tsunami forming in NS?' narrative (ESC-013, MEDIUM), though it is structurally counteracted by the Bersatu-PN split. NET: the PH-favorable tilt STRENGTHENS and is now narrative-backed; the opposition fracture is the dominant, defining development of Nomination Day. Distribution: 10 positive / 9 neutral / 6 negative (full-day merged; baseline was 4/4/4).

[19:19Δ REVISION 3 — CYCLE 3 DELTA MERGED] The 19:19 MYT collection cycle (raw-scrape _111951 batch + nomination-day-summary-1919) extends the Nomination-Day verdict along three axes: (1) The Bersatu-PN split moves TOWARD FORMALIZATION — NST (Kiandee): 'PN has grounds to remove Bersatu.' The termination threat has acquired concrete 'grounds' (progression: 'could face' -> 'info chief floats' -> 'has grounds'), making this the strongest signal yet that ESC-010/ESC-011 may formalize into an actual procedural expulsion during the campaign window — the single highest-priority watch item, adjacent to trigger #2. The split is now THREE-WAY: Bersatu vs (PN+PAS) (Hadi, JEMPOL). NEW ESC-014 (CRITICAL) raised; ESC-010/ESC-011 updated to 'toward formalization'. (2) Zahid Hamidi ELEVATES the NS strategic stakes to a bellwether/realignment determinant — 'new political alignments needed' (Star, JEMPOL) + 'PRN NS prestasi BN-PN mungkin jadi penentu jajaran baharu PRN Melaka, PRU16' (Astro Awani). The NS contest is now publicly framed as a determinant of future national coalition restructuring (NEW NAR-19). (3) PH's edge gains a MACHINERY/mobilisation dimension — R. Ramanan (PKR VP, Sungai Buloh MP) deployed to the Wisma MBS nomination centre to back 6 PH candidates (The Star, SEREMBAN), the first concrete nomination-centre mobilisation datum of the day. PH's edge is now STRUCTURAL + NARRATIVE (rev 2) + MACHINERY (rev 3). NST independently corroborates the EC's 21 three-cornered + 2 five-way figure (N.10 Nilai, N.33 Sri Tanjung) — PIR-04 CONFIRMED. No escalation trigger has FIRED, but trigger #2 (coalition restructuring) is elevated to WATCH (ELEVATED — HIGHEST PRIORITY), the closest it has been. NET: the PH-favorable tilt STRENGTHENS further; the opposition fracture is escalating toward a formal mid-campaign restructuring event. Distribution: 11 positive / 9 neutral / 6 negative (revision-3 merged; rev-2 was 10/9/6; baseline 4/4/4).
